ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists for honey cake specifically or for a cake-plus-beverage set meal. FOODON_00002426 (cake food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cakes, pastries and similar sweet baked goods
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers sweet baked goods broadly; no specific honey cake or set-meal code exists. Used as closest parent.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for honey cake or café set meal. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.description: 1 set (1 slice honey cake + 1 medium hot beverage)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one slice of honey cake (approximately 80–100 g) accompanied by one medium-sized hot beverage (approximately 220–240 ml), totalling roughly 320 g combined weight.

nutrient_source.title: Castella sponge cake / honey cake — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Cake, sponge (NDB 18101) combined with honey component adjustment; hot beverage modelled as black coffee/tea (negligible macros)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Toast Box Honey Cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Nutrients are adapted from USDA SR Legacy sponge cake (NDB 18101) with upward adjustment for honey content (~10% substitution of sugar by honey, adding ~5 kcal/100g and marginally increasing sugar_g). The hot beverage component (black coffee or kopi/teh) contributes negligible macronutrients at medium serving size and is modelled as zero-macro contribution to the per-100g composite. Values represent the cake portion per 100 g as the dominant nutritional component of the set.

gi_source.title: Sponge cake GI reference — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Toast Box Honey Cake. GI of 65 is estimated from sponge cake GI values reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables (sponge cake GI range 46–66, mean ~65). Honey substitution marginally raises GI relative to plain sponge; 65 is used as a conservative upper-range estimate. GL per serving calculated as (65 × 48.4 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 31.5, where serving carbohydrate = 60.5 g/100g × 80 g cake portion = 48.4 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~185 mg/100g) and high sugar load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one small slice and choose a low-sodium hot beverage such as plain tea, as the combined sodium and high refined-sugar content can contribute to fluid retention and elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (65) and high sugar content (~38 g sugar/100g) with significant glycemic load per serving (~31.5)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this set as an occasional indulgence only; the high sugar and medium-to-high GI of honey cake produce a substantial glycemic load per serving (~31.5), requiring careful carbohydrate budgeting and blood glucose monitoring post-consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.8 g/100g), cholesterol (~148 mg/100g), and high refined sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should consume this set sparingly, as the combination of saturated fat, dietary cholesterol from eggs, and high added sugar can adversely affect LDL cholesterol and triglyceride levels when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Honey Cake Set with Medium Hot Beverage is a popular café set meal offered at Toast Box, a Singaporean kopitiam-style chain. The set consists of a slice of honey cake — a soft, lightly sweetened sponge cake made with wheat flour, eggs, butter, sugar, and honey — paired with a medium-sized hot beverage, typically kopi (traditional Singaporean coffee brewed with a sock filter and condensed milk), kopi-o (black coffee with sugar), teh (milk tea), or teh-o (black tea with sugar). The honey cake has a moist, tender crumb with a delicate honey aroma and a golden-brown crust. It is baked rather than fried, giving it a lighter texture compared to richer Western butter cakes. Nutritionally, the cake portion is energy-dense (~327 kcal/100g) and high in refined carbohydrates and sugars (~60.5 g carbohydrate, ~38 g sugar per 100g), with moderate protein (~7.1 g) from eggs and a modest fat content (~6.2 g). The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium-high), producing a substantial glycemic load per serving. Clinically, this set is of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to its high sugar and glycemic load, and for those with cardiovascular disease due to its saturated fat and cholesterol content from eggs and butter. It is best consumed as an occasional treat rather than a daily dietary staple. The set is a culturally significant comfort food in Singapore's kopitiam tradition, commonly enjoyed as a morning or afternoon snack.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Toast Box honey cake?
What is in a Toast Box set meal?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Toast Box Honey Cake exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods. Nutrients are adapted from USDA SR Legacy sponge cake (NDB 18101) with honey-content adjustment. GI is estimated from analogous sponge cake values in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the adaptation basis is well-documented and sponge cake is a close compositional match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent the cake component per 100g; the hot beverage (black coffee or tea base) contributes negligible macronutrients and is not separately itemised in per_100g. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy sponge cake source entry. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy sponge cake source entry. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy sponge cake source entry. 5. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero — sponge cake contains no vitamin C per USDA FoodData Central. 6. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from sponge cake GI data (University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008); no direct measurement for Toast Box Honey Cake exists. 7. Halal status is assumed based on Toast Box's general halal certification in Singapore; kosher status is not confirmed. 8. The beverage component of the set (kopi, teh, etc.) may add sugar and condensed milk calories if ordered with milk/sugar; this is not reflected in the per_100g cake-only values.
